skill.name: chatcrystal-task-recall
skill.description: Recall project-first and global-supplement ChatCrystal memories before substantive implementation, refactoring, migration, configuration, investigation, or optimization work. Use when the task is non-trivial, has repository or project context, and prior fixes, decisions, pitfalls, or reusable patterns may change the approach.
skill.summary: Recall project-specific memories and patterns before substantial code changes
skill.author: ZengLiangYi

security_audit.summary: Static analysis detected 27 potential issues, all confirmed as false positives after review. The 'weak cryptographic algorithm' and 'external commands' findings were triggered by markdown code blocks and file path names, not actual executable code. This skill contains only documentation and workflow instructions with no implementation code, presenting no security risk.

content.value_statement: Avoid repeating mistakes and forgetting past decisions. This skill automatically recalls relevant project memories, patterns, and pitfalls before you make substantial changes to your codebase.
actual_capabilities: Recalls project-specific memories before substantial work using MCP tool integration
Provides structured workflow for implementation, refactoring, and optimization tasks
Surfaces relevant pitfalls, patterns, and prior decisions from project history
Supports both full mode with ChatCrystal Core and degraded mode without blocking
Integrates with repository context including branches and related files
Distinguishes between project memories and global supplemental memories
limitations: Requires ChatCrystal Core and MCP access to recall_for_task for full functionality
Only works when task has repository or project context
Skips trivial edits and one-line answers by design
Returns nothing useful if no prior project memories exist
Cannot create new memories, only recall existing ones

use_cases.title: Implement Complex Features
use_cases.description: Before adding new endpoints or features, recall prior architectural decisions and reusable patterns from the project history.

use_cases.title: Refactor Legacy Code
use_cases.description: Before refactoring, recall past pitfalls and successful patterns used in similar refactoring work within this project.

use_cases.title: Debug Recurring Issues
use_cases.description: When investigating bugs, recall previous fixes and decisions related to the failing components to avoid repeating mistakes.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Recall Before Implementation
prompt_templates.prompt: Use chatcrystal-task-recall to recall prior project memories before implementing this feature.
prompt_templates.scenario: Starting a new implementation task in an existing project

prompt_templates.title: Refactoring With Context
prompt_templates.prompt: Use chatcrystal-task-recall to recall relevant patterns and pitfalls before refactoring the authentication module.

prompt_templates.title: Investigation With History
prompt_templates.prompt: Use chatcrystal-task-recall with mode 'task' and task_kind 'investigate' to recall prior decisions about this component before debugging.
prompt_templates.scenario: Investigating a bug or issue in existing code

prompt_templates.title: Migration With Patterns
prompt_templates.prompt: Use chatcrystal-task-recall to recall migration patterns and prior decisions before upgrading the database schema.
prompt_templates.scenario: Planning a migration or major configuration change

output_examples.input: Use chatcrystal-task-recall before implementing the user export feature
output_examples.output: Recalled 3 project memories: Prior pagination pattern from routes/notes.ts, previous export performance issue with large datasets, and decision to use streaming for file downloads. Applying these patterns to the new user export endpoint.

output_examples.input: Use chatcrystal-task-recall before refactoring the error handling
output_examples.output: Recalled 2 project memories: Standardized error response format from shared/types/index.ts and previous pitfall about not exposing stack traces. Will follow the established error pattern.

best_practices: Always use recall before substantial implementation, refactoring, or optimization work
Provide complete task context including project_dir, branch, and related_files for best results
Treat project memories as primary context and global memories as supplemental
Continue work normally if recall returns no useful matches rather than blocking
anti_patterns: Do not use recall for trivial one-line edits or purely conversational requests
Do not invent a parallel memory system within the skill itself
Do not claim memory was recalled when ChatCrystal Core is unavailable in degraded mode
Do not instruct the runtime to auto-install ChatCrystal Core as part of skill execution

faq.question: What does this skill actually do?
faq.answer: This skill provides a workflow to recall project-specific memories, patterns, and pitfalls before you make substantial changes to code. It uses an MCP tool called recall_for_task to surface relevant historical context.

faq.question: Do I need to install anything?
faq.answer: Full functionality requires ChatCrystal Core and the MCP recall_for_task tool. However, the skill includes a degraded mode that continues work without blocking if these are unavailable.

faq.question: When should I use this skill?
faq.answer: Use this skill before non-trivial implementation, refactoring, migration, configuration, investigation, or optimization work. Skip it for quick edits or simple questions.

faq.question: What is the difference between project and global memories?
faq.answer: Project memories are specific to the current repository or workspace. Global memories are supplemental patterns and knowledge that apply across projects. The skill treats project memories as primary context.

faq.question: What happens if no memories are found?
faq.answer: The skill continues the task normally without blocking. It may surface warnings like no-project-key or no-matches, but these are not treated as failures.

faq.question: Can I use this skill without Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes, this skill works with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code. However, it requires the MCP recall_for_task tool to be available in your environment for full functionality.
